title: Proclamation No. 55, s. 1936
text:
MALACAÑAN PALACE MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 55

REVOKING PROCLAMATION NO. 270, SERIES OF 1929, AND ESTABLISHING AND DESIGNATING AS THE ALBAY-SORSOGON FOREST RESERVE FOR FOREST PROTECTION AND TIMBER PRODUCTION, THE PARCEL OF PUBLIC DOMAIN SITUATED IN THE MUNICIPALITIES OF ALBAY AND MANITO, PROVINCE OF ALBAY, AND MUNICIPALITIES OF CASTILLA, SORSOGON, AND BACON, PROVINCE OF SORSOGON, ISLAND OF LUZON.

Upon the recommendation of the Director of Forestry, approved by the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce, I, Manuel L. Quezon, President of the Philippines, hereby revoke Proclamation Numbered Two hundred and seventy-nine, series of nineteen hundred and twenty-nine, classifying as timber land parcels of the public domain situated in the Municipalities of Albay and Manito, Province of Albay, and Municipalities of Sorsogon, Bacon, and Castilla, Province of Sorsogon, Island of Luzon; and pursuant to the provisions of section eighteen hundred and twenty-six of Act Numbered twenty-seven hundred and eleven, known as the Revised Administrative Code, I hereby establish the Albay-Sorsogon Forest Reserve and place it under the administration and control of the Bureau of Forestry, which shall have the authority to regulate the cutting, collection, and removal of timber and other forest products therein in accordance with the Forest Law and Regulations for forest protection and timber production, subject to private rights, if any there be, and to any and all rights and privileges of exploration, occupation, purchase, or otherwise granted or reserved to citizens by provisions of Act of Congress of July first, nineteen hundred and two, known as the Philippine Bill.

I, therefore, withdraw from sale or settlement, except as permitted or provided for in the aforesaid Act of Congress of July first, nineteen hundred and two, and hereby declare and proclaim to be the Albay-Sorsogon Forest Reserve that portion of the public domain, located in the Municipalities of Albay and Manito, Province of Albay, and Municipalities of Castilla, Sorsogon, and Bacon, Province of Sorsogon, Island of Luzon, and described in the Bureau of Forestry map No. F. R. 60, to wit:
